name: Inventory Turnover
description: How many times average inventory is sold and replaced over a period, measured as cost of goods sold divided by average inventory value.

outputs.name: Inventory turnover
outputs.description: Times inventory turns over in the period.

assumptions: COGS and average inventory are valued on a consistent basis (both at cost).
Average inventory represents the period (e.g. average of opening and closing).
Days inventory outstanding follows as period_days / turns.
limitations: Sales-based turnover (revenue / inventory) inflates the ratio; use COGS for a like-for-like measure.
Seasonal businesses need a representative average, not a single snapshot.

examples.note: Distribution Planning review problem: COGS US$150M, average inventory US$30M.

citation.quote: Cost of goods is US$150 million; Average inventory is US$30 million ... calculate the inventory turnover ratio.

citations.quote: turnover is given by Annual usage x Unit cost.
7.5.3 Inventory turns and ratios ... financial measurements of inventory.
